browser.title: Browser
browser.empty: No saved services or recent projects yet.
browser.addFailed: Could not add this data source.
browser.favoriteMissing: That data source no longer exists — removed from Favorites.

about.title: About GeoLibre
about.description: GeoLibre is a lightweight cloud-native GIS platform.

updates.noChangelog: Release notes are not available. See the downloads page for details.

steps.download: Download the installer for your platform from the downloads page.
steps.install: Run the installer. Your local projects, settings, and API keys are kept.

error.rateLimit: GitHub rate limit exceeded. Please try again later.
error.noTag: The latest release does not include a version tag.
error.network: Could not reach GitHub. Check your connection and try again.
error.viewDownloadsHint: Opens the GeoLibre downloads page in your browser so you can check the latest version manually.

startup.title: Update available
startup.settingsHint: You can change update checks in Settings → Updates.

bookmark.captureStateLabel: Include complete layer state (active, inactive, and layer order)
bookmark.captureStateTooltip: Applies to the bookmark you save next, not as a global setting. Leave it on to restore exactly this layer arrangement later.

trust.title: Load plugins from this project?
trust.description_one: This project wants to load 1 external plugin from a URL you have not installed. Plugins run code with full access to the app.
trust.description_other: This project wants to load {{count}} external plugins from URLs you have not installed. Plugins run code with full access to the app.
trust.warning: Only load plugins from sources you trust. Loaded plugins run with the same privileges as GeoLibre itself.

terrainMeasure.title: Terrain (3D)
terrainMeasure.partialData: Some samples had no terrain data

fileNamePrompt.title: Save file as
fileNamePrompt.description: Enter a file name. The file downloads to your browser's downloads folder.
fileNamePrompt.label: File name

newProject.basemapDescription: Choose an OpenFreeMap or Protomaps basemap, a blank background, or a MapLibre style URL.
newProject.basemapDescriptionNoProtomaps: Choose an OpenFreeMap basemap, a blank background, or a MapLibre style URL.
newProject.savePromptDescription: The current project has unsaved changes. Save them before creating a new project?

basemapPicker.title: Change basemap
basemapPicker.invalidUrl: Enter a valid HTTP or HTTPS style URL.

common.pickColorFromScreen: Pick a color from the screen

xyz.label: Add XYZ Layer
xyz.description: Add a raster tile template using x, y, and z placeholders.

wms.label: Add WMS Layer
wms.description: Add a WMS GetMap service as a tiled raster layer.

wfs.label: Add WFS Layer
wfs.description: Add WFS features by requesting GeoJSON from a GetFeature service.

wmts.label: Add WMTS Layer
wmts.description: Add a WMTS tile URL template as a raster layer.

ogcVectorTiles.label: Add OGC Vector Tiles Layer
ogcVectorTiles.description: Add an OGC API - Tiles (vector) service from a TileJSON or tile template, optionally reading its source layers from a style URL.

gpx.label: Add GPX Layer
gpx.description: Add GPX waypoints, routes, and tracks as one or more vector layers.

georss.label: Add GeoRSS Layer
georss.description: Add a GeoRSS feed (RSS, Atom, or RDF) from a URL or file as a vector layer.

delimitedText.label: Add Delimited Text Layer
delimitedText.description: Add a delimited text file or URL as a point layer using longitude and latitude fields, or as a non-spatial attribute table when no coordinates are available.

cad.label: Add CAD (DXF/DWG) Layer
cad.description: Add an AutoCAD DXF or DWG drawing as a vector layer, choosing which layer to load and the coordinate reference system to project it from.

photos.label: Add Geotagged Photos
photos.description: Import JPEG/HEIC photos as a point layer, placing each one from its EXIF GPS location.

mbtiles.label: Add MBTiles Layer
mbtiles.description: Add a local MBTiles file as a raster or vector tile layer.

arcgis.label: Add ArcGIS Layer
arcgis.description: Add an ArcGIS FeatureServer layer, VectorTileServer layer, or portal item.

postgres.label: Add PostgreSQL Layer
postgres.description: Start Martin locally, discover PostGIS sources, and add a source as vector tiles.

deckglViz.label: Add Deck.gl Layer
deckglViz.description: Pick a deck.gl layer type, then load the example data or upload a CSV/JSON/GeoJSON file or URL.

video.label: Add Video Layer
video.description: Add a georeferenced video overlay by supplying an MP4 URL and four corner coordinates.

common.networkFailure: Could not reach the service. Check that the host is reachable and that its TLS certificate, firewall, and proxy settings allow the request; in the browser it may also be blocked by CORS, so try the desktop app.
common.requestTimedOut: The request timed out. The host may be slow, unreachable, or blocked by a firewall or proxy.

serviceLibrary.title: Saved services
serviceLibrary.empty: No saved services yet — fill in the form and choose “Save current”.
serviceLibrary.name: Name
serviceLibrary.errorName: Enter a name for the saved service.
serviceLibrary.errorTooLarge: File is too large to import (max 5 MB).
serviceLibrary.errorNoServices: No valid services found in that file.
serviceLibrary.errorRead: Could not read that file as a service library.

xyz.errorUrl: Enter an XYZ tile URL template.

wms.errorLayers: Enter one or more WMS layer names.
wms.noLayersFound: No layers were found for this service.
wms.retrieveError: Could not retrieve layers from this service.

wfs.errorTypeName: Enter a WFS feature type name.
wfs.errorMaxFeaturesNumeric: Enter a numeric max features value.
wfs.noTypesFound: No feature types were found for this service.
wfs.retrieveError: Could not retrieve feature types from this service.

wmts.errorUrl: Enter a WMTS tile URL template.

ogcVectorTiles.tilesUrlHint: A TileJSON metadata URL or a {z}/{y}/{x} MVT tile template.
ogcVectorTiles.styleUrlHint: Optional Mapbox/MapLibre style JSON. Used to discover the tileset's source layers when the TileJSON omits them.
ogcVectorTiles.sourceLayersHint: Optional comma-separated source layer names. Overrides the layers found in the TileJSON or style.
ogcVectorTiles.errorUrl: Enter a tiles URL or a style URL.
ogcVectorTiles.errorNoTiles: Could not determine the vector tile endpoint. Enter a TileJSON URL, a tile template, or a style URL.
ogcVectorTiles.errorNoLayers: Could not determine the vector tile source layers. Add a style URL or enter the source layer names.

gpx.errorSelectType: Select at least one GPX layer type.
gpx.errorNotFound: The selected GPX layer types were not found.

cad.crsHelp: Leave blank if the drawing is already in longitude/latitude (WGS84). Otherwise enter the EPSG code of the drawing's coordinate system to reproject it.
cad.readError: Could not read this CAD file.
cad.errorNoLayers: No layers were found in this CAD file.
cad.errorNoLayer: Select a CAD layer to load.
cad.errorUnsupportedGeometry: This CAD layer contains mixed or collection geometry that cannot be displayed. Try another layer.

delimitedText.errorFileMissing: Delimited text file data is missing.
delimitedText.readError: Could not read delimited text file.
delimitedText.retrievedColumnsTable_one: Retrieved {{count}} column. No coordinate columns detected; it will be added as an attribute table.
delimitedText.retrievedColumnsTable_other: Retrieved {{count}} columns. No coordinate columns detected; it will be added as an attribute table.
delimitedText.tableHint: Leave both coordinate fields as None to add the file as a non-spatial attribute table (for use in an attribute join).

photos.hint: Select JPEG, PNG, TIFF, or HEIC photos. Each one is placed from its EXIF GPS location. HEIC photos are located but shown without a thumbnail.
photos.readError: Could not read the selected photos.
photos.errorNoGps_one: No GPS location was found in the selected photo.
photos.errorNoGps_other: No GPS location was found in the {{count}} selected photos.
photos.noThumbnailNote_one: {{count}} photo has no thumbnail (HEIC or unsupported format).
photos.noThumbnailNote_other: {{count}} photos have no thumbnail (HEIC or unsupported format).
photos.manualPromptTitle: No GPS location was found in this photo.
photos.manualPromptBody: Place it at the current map center and drag it into position on the map.
photos.manualHint: Drag this pin to position the photo, then click Done.

mbtiles.errorSourceLayers: Enter at least one vector source layer.

postgres.statusNoSources: Martin is running, but no compatible PostGIS sources were found.
postgres.errorNoVectorLayers: The selected Martin source has no vector layers.
postgres.errorSelectSource: Select a Martin source to add.
postgres.desktopOnlyNotice: PostgreSQL layers are only available in GeoLibre Desktop. This feature runs a local Martin tile server, which the web app cannot launch.
postgres.editableNotice: Loads the table's features through the local processing server so they can be edited. Save changes back with the layer's "Save edits to PostGIS table" action.
postgres.statusNoTables: No spatial tables were found in this database.
postgres.errorRuntimeMissing: The processing server does not have the PostGIS runtime (psycopg) installed. Install the geolibre-server 'postgis' extra to load editable layers.

video.errorHttps: Video URLs must start with https://.
video.errorCornerNumber: Enter a numeric {{corner}} longitude and latitude.
video.cornersNote: The four corners georeference the video on the map. The video must be served over HTTPS and the host must allow cross-origin requests (CORS) for the frames to render.

netcdf.title: Add NetCDF / HDF
netcdf.description: Render a NetCDF or HDF5 dataset: a remote Cloud-Optimized file via a kerchunk reference (read over HTTP range requests), or a local NetCDF or HDF5 file read in your browser.
netcdf.localFileHelp: Select a local NetCDF or HDF5 file (.nc, .nc4, .cdf, .h5, or .hdf5); classic NetCDF-3 and NetCDF-4/HDF5 are both supported. It is decoded in your browser; large files use more memory.
netcdf.errorNoVariables: No renderable (2-D or higher) variables found in the file.

deckViz.errorChooseFile: Choose a CSV, JSON, or GeoJSON file.
deckViz.needsTabular: {{label}} needs tabular CSV/JSON data, not GeoJSON.
deckViz.errorExample: Could not load the example data.
deckViz.errorLoadFirst: Load the example data, or a file/URL, first.

offline.title: Download Offline Area
offline.description: Save the current map view's basemap tiles so this area keeps working without a network connection.
offline.noServiceWorker: Offline caching needs the installed web app. Tiles you download here won't be saved for offline use in this build.
offline.uncacheable: The following basemap sources don't support offline downloading and will be missing from your offline map: {{hosts}}.
offline.cacheable: The following basemap sources will be downloaded and available offline: {{hosts}}.
offline.includeExtra: Include extra detail levels for zooming in
offline.tooManyTiles: This area exceeds the offline cache limit ({{max}} resources); the oldest cached tiles may be evicted as newer resources download. Reduce the detail levels or pick a smaller area.
offline.acknowledgeEviction: Download anyway, evicting older tiles if needed
offline.complete_one: Offline area saved. {{count}} resource cached for offline use.
offline.complete_other: Offline area saved. {{count}} resources cached for offline use.
offline.concurrencyHint: Lower this to ease off servers that rate-limit rapid tile requests.
offline.timeoutHint: Each tile request is given up to {{seconds}}s before it counts as failed.
offline.timeoutDisabled: No timeout — requests use the browser default. Raise it for slow connections.

offlineManager.title: Manage Offline Areas
offlineManager.description: Review the basemap areas you've downloaded for offline use, refresh them, or delete them to reclaim storage.
offlineManager.noServiceWorker: Offline caching needs the installed web app. Refreshing areas won't save tiles for offline use in this build.
offlineManager.empty: No offline areas yet. Use "Download Offline Area" to save the current map view for offline use.

recordTour.title: Record Map Tour
recordTour.hint: Pan and zoom the map, then capture each view. The map stays interactive while this panel is open.
recordTour.unsupported: This browser cannot record the map canvas. Try a recent version of Chrome, Edge, or Firefox.
recordTour.pathNoLayers: Add a LineString layer to generate keyframes from a path.
recordTour.pathConfirmReplace: Replace the current keyframes with keyframes generated from this path?
recordTour.pathGenerateError: Could not generate keyframes from this path.
recordTour.confirmLoad: Replace the current keyframes with the loaded setup?
recordTour.configSaveError: Could not save the tour setup. Please try again.
recordTour.configLoadError: Could not load the tour setup. The file may be invalid.
recordTour.empty: Pan and zoom the map to a starting view, then add it as the first keyframe. Add at least two keyframes to record a tour.
recordTour.holdSeconds: Seconds to hold on this view
recordTour.segmentSeconds: Seconds to move to the next view
recordTour.resizeLeft: Resize panel from the bottom-left corner
recordTour.resizeRight: Resize panel from the bottom-right corner
recordTour.recordingReady: Recording ready. Name it and save, or discard to re-record.
recordTour.recordError: Could not record the tour. Please try again.
recordTour.saveError: Could not save the video. Please try again.

recordVideo.title: Record Video
recordVideo.hint: Record the live map as a video. Pan and zoom while recording — every move is captured. This panel and the selection frame are not part of the video.
recordVideo.unsupported: This browser cannot record the map canvas. Try a recent version of Chrome, Edge, or Firefox.
recordVideo.drawHint: Drag a rectangle on the map to set the recording area, or press Escape to cancel.
recordVideo.regionChosen: Recording area: {{width}} × {{height}} px. The frame stays fixed while the map moves under it.
recordVideo.noRegion: Draw a rectangle on the map to choose the recording area.
recordVideo.needRegion: Select an area on the map first.
recordVideo.recordingReady: Recording ready. Name it and save, or discard to re-record.
recordVideo.recordError: Could not record the video. Please try again.
recordVideo.saveError: Could not save the video. Please try again.

fieldCollection.title: Field Collection
fieldCollection.description: Capture point, line, or polygon observations against a custom form. Place a point by GPS or by tapping the map, and draw lines and polygons vertex by vertex.
fieldCollection.captureDescription: Capture a location by GPS or on the map, then fill in the form to save it.
fieldCollection.captureReviewDescription: Review the captured location, complete the form, and save it to the layer.
fieldCollection.noFields: No fields yet. Add fields to capture attributes, or create the layer to capture location-only points.
fieldCollection.captureHint: Use GPS or pick a point on the map to start a capture.
fieldCollection.drawHint: Draw the shape on the map to start a capture.
fieldCollection.pickBannerHint: Click anywhere on the map to pick a point, or press Esc to cancel.
fieldCollection.dblClickHint: Tap to add vertices; double-click to finish.
fieldCollection.layerGone: The collection layer is no longer available.
fieldCollection.noGeolocation: Geolocation isn't available on this device.
fieldCollection.geolocationDenied: Couldn't get your location. Check location permissions and try again.
fieldCollection.photoTooLarge: That photo is too large (max {{max}}). Choose a smaller image.
fieldCollection.photoReadError: Couldn't read that image. Try another file.
fieldCollection.errorChoice: Choose one of the listed options.

georeferencer.title: Georeference Image
georeferencer.description: Pin an image to the map: load it, link image points to map locations, then add it as a georeferenced overlay.
georeferencer.loadImage: Click to load an image (PNG, JPG, TIFF)
georeferencer.imageTooLarge: That image is too large (max {{max}}). Choose a smaller image.
georeferencer.imageReadError: Couldn't read that image. Try another file.
georeferencer.imageNoDimensions: Couldn't determine the image size. Use a raster image (PNG, JPG, TIFF).
georeferencer.clickImageHint: Click a point on the image, then link it on the map.
georeferencer.gcpsReadError: Couldn't read control points from that file.
georeferencer.gcpsOutsideImage: Some control points fall outside the loaded image — they may be from a different image.
georeferencer.transformNeedsMore: This transform needs at least {{min}} control points.
georeferencer.needGcps: Add at least {{min}} control points to georeference.
georeferencer.collinear: Control points are collinear or coincident — move one off the line.
georeferencer.cornersOutOfRange: This fit places the image outside valid map bounds. Adjust the control points.
georeferencer.exportTiffHint: Warps the image to a Cloud-Optimized GeoTIFF in your browser (GDAL/WASM). The engine downloads on first use.
georeferencer.exporting: Exporting GeoTIFF… (loading GDAL on first use)
georeferencer.exportFailed: GeoTIFF export failed. See the console for details.

collaborate.title: Live collaboration
collaborate.description: Share a live session so others can see and edit this map with you in real time.
collaborate.invitedDescription: Pick your name and color, then join the host's live session.
collaborate.connectFailed: Could not connect to the collaboration server.
collaborate.sessionStatusTooltip: Live collaboration session. Click to see who's connected.
collaborate.codeRequired: Enter a session code to join.
collaborate.sessionStatusUnread_one: Live collaboration session, {{count}} unread message. Click to see who's connected and chat.
collaborate.sessionStatusUnread_other: Live collaboration session, {{count}} unread messages. Click to see who's connected and chat.

share.title: Share project
share.description: Upload the current project to share.geolibre.app and get a shareable link.
share.setupIntro: Connect your share.geolibre.app account before uploading.
share.step1Description: Sign in to share.geolibre.app, then create a token under Settings → API tokens.
share.step2Description: Paste the token into Settings → Environment Variables on this device.
share.titleRequired: Enter a project title before sharing.
share.usernameRequired: Set a username on your share.geolibre.app account before sharing. Open your account settings to choose one, then try again.

gallery.title: Project gallery
gallery.description: Browse public projects shared on share.geolibre.app and open one in GeoLibre.
gallery.searchPlaceholder: Filter loaded projects by title, author, or tag
gallery.empty: No public projects to show yet.
gallery.noMatches: No loaded projects match your filter.
gallery.errorFallback: Could not load the project gallery.
gallery.errorTimeout: The project gallery timed out. Please try again.
gallery.errorNetwork: Could not reach share.geolibre.app. Check your internet connection.
gallery.errorInvalidResponse: Could not load the gallery (invalid server response).
gallery.errorUnauthorized: Your share.geolibre.app API token is invalid or expired. Update it in Settings.
gallery.errorUsernameRequired: Set a username on your share.geolibre.app account before loading your projects.
gallery.signedOutHint: Add a share.geolibre.app API token in Settings to see your own unlisted and private projects.
gallery.emptyMine: You have not shared any projects yet.

language.label: Language
language.description: Choose the language used for the interface.

printLayout.title: Print Layout
printLayout.description: Compose a print-ready map with a title, legend, scale bar, and north arrow, then export it to PNG or PDF.

extent.label: Print extent
extent.hint: Drag a box on the map to set the exact print area. Hold Shift to match the page proportions.

errors.mapNotReady: Map is not ready yet. Try again in a moment.
errors.captureFailed: Could not capture the map image.
errors.clipboardFailed: Could not copy the layout to the clipboard.
errors.scaleOutOfRange: This scale is outside the range the current view can reach; showing the closest available scale.

legend.empty: No visible layers to include in the legend.

map.boundsRestrictedTooltip: Map bounds are restricted. You can change this in Settings.

directionsMode.title: Directions mode active
directionsMode.hint: Click the map to add waypoints. Drag a waypoint to move it, or click it to remove it. Routes update automatically.

reverseGeocodeMode.title: Reverse Geocode mode active
reverseGeocodeMode.hint: Click anywhere on the map to look up the address at that point.

pixelTimeSeriesMode.title: Pixel time series mode active
pixelTimeSeriesMode.hint: Click pixels on the raster stack to add them to the chart. Esc to stop.

terrainSettings.title: Terrain exaggeration
terrainSettings.description: Adjust the vertical exaggeration of the 3D terrain. Higher values make hills and mountains appear steeper.
terrainSettings.label: Vertical exaggeration

pixelTimeSeries.title: Pixel time series
pixelTimeSeries.empty: Click a pixel on the map to plot its value over time. Add more pixels to compare.
pixelTimeSeries.querying: Reading pixel values across the timeline...
pixelTimeSeries.noValues: No values for the selected band at these locations (outside the raster or all timesteps are nodata).
pixelTimeSeries.truncated: Showing {{kept}} of {{total}} timeline steps (downsampled to limit reads).
pixelTimeSeries.chartAria: Line chart of pixel value over time

rasterSubset.title: Extract subset
rasterSubset.drawHint: Click and drag on the map to draw the extract area. Press Esc to cancel.
rasterSubset.bboxHint: Enter a valid box: west/east within -180 to 180, south/north within -90 to 90, with west < east and south < north.
rasterSubset.bboxAntimeridianHint: West must be less than East. Boxes that cross the 180° meridian aren't supported; split them into two extracts.
rasterSubset.zoomHint: Enter a whole zoom level between 0 and 30.
rasterSubset.resolutionHint: Output pixel size in the output CRS units. Leave blank to keep the source resolution.
rasterSubset.additionalArgsHint: One key=value per line, e.g. level, width, height.
rasterSubset.errorResolution: Resolution must be a positive number.
rasterSubset.errorOutputCrs: Output CRS must be a positive EPSG code.
rasterSubset.errorAdditionalArgs: Additional options must be one key=value per line.

knowledgeCard.title: Place info
knowledgeCard.empty: No Wikipedia articles found near this place.
knowledgeCard.error: Could not load place info. Please try again.
knowledgeCard.attribution: Content from Wikipedia, licensed CC BY-SA.
knowledgeCard.noticeDesc: Opening a knowledge card sends the coordinates of the place you clicked (and the title of any nearby article you open) to Wikipedia's public API to fetch a summary and photo — your coordinates leave your device for those requests.

onboarding.title: Welcome to GeoLibre
onboarding.description: Choose your experience level to tailor the interface. You can change this any time in Settings → Interface.

beginner.title: Beginner
beginner.description: Show only the essential data sources and tools.

intermediate.title: Intermediate
intermediate.description: Show common data sources, services, and plugins.

advanced.title: Advanced
advanced.description: Show everything GeoLibre has to offer.

settings.title: Settings
settings.description: Configure project preferences and runtime settings.

menu.urlOverrideNote: URL layout parameters override saved settings.

updates.title: Software Updates
updates.description: Control how GeoLibre checks for new versions when the desktop app starts.
updates.checkOnStartupHint: When enabled, GeoLibre checks for a newer version each time the app starts. Turn this off for a fully offline workflow.
updates.notificationLevelHint: Choose which kinds of releases trigger a startup notification.

map.useCurrentViewHint: Captures the current map extent and turns on Restrict map bounds.
map.useCurrentViewGlobeHint: Switch to the Mercator projection before using this. In the Globe projection the map can still drift slightly beyond the captured bounds.
map.ellipsoidHint: Sets the radius used for distance, area, and scale measurements. Pick a matching planetary basemap under Add Data.
map.scaleUnitHint: Choose the units shown on the scale bar at the bottom of the map.
map.errorBoundsUnavailable: The map bounds are not available yet.

layout.title: Layout
layout.showProjectInfoToolbar: Show project info in the toolbar
layout.urlParamsNote: URL layout parameters still apply when present in shared viewer links.

appearance.title: Appearance
appearance.description: Choose a light or dark mode and an accent color that applies on top of it.

interface.title: Interface profile
interface.description: Choose an experience level to simplify the interface. Nothing is removed, so you can switch levels or re-enable items here at any time.
interface.presetsHint: Beginner, Intermediate, and Advanced apply a curated layout instantly. Editing any item below switches to Custom, or click Custom to keep the current setup in custom mode.
interface.lockedNote: This interface profile is managed by your administrator and cannot be changed.

env.tokenDescription: Project > Share uses this token to upload your maps to share.geolibre.app. Open <tokenLink>share.geolibre.app/settings</tokenLink>, create a token under Settings > API tokens, then paste it into the field below.
env.tokenStorageNote: Stored locally on this device and sent only to share.geolibre.app to authenticate uploads. On the web build it shares the same browser storage as other site data, so revoke it on share.geolibre.app if your machine is compromised.
env.cesiumTokenDescription: The 3D globe (a split-view pane) uses Cesium Ion world imagery and terrain, which need an access token. Create a free account at <tokenLink>ion.cesium.com/tokens</tokenLink>, copy your default access token, and paste it below. Without a token the 3D globe toggle is hidden.
env.cesiumTokenStorageNote: Stored locally on this device (not in the project file, so it is never shared) and sent only to Cesium Ion. On the web build it shares the same browser storage as other site data.
env.secretsWarning: Values are stored in plain text in the project file and may be exposed when the project is shared. Avoid putting secrets here unless the project file stays private.
env.errorNamePattern: Environment variable names must start with a letter or underscore and contain only letters, numbers, and underscores.
env.stripPromptDesc: This project has {{count}} environment variable(s) (which may include API keys). They are stored in plain text in the project file and could be exposed if you share it. Remove them from the saved file? Your Settings keep them on this device either way.

geocoding.title: Geocoding
geocoding.description: Choose the geocoding backend used by Geocode Addresses and Reverse Geocode. Nominatim is the default and needs no key.
geocoding.requiresKey: This provider needs an API key or access token.
geocoding.optionalKey: The hosted service needs an API key; a self-hosted instance does not.
geocoding.noKey: This provider works without an API key.
geocoding.apiKeyPlaceholder: Paste the provider API key or token
geocoding.emailHint: Only used by Nominatim, which recommends providing it to help identify your application — sent only when provided.
geocoding.secretsWarning: Keys are stored in plain text in the project file and may be exposed when the project is shared. They are also sent as query parameters, so they appear in geocoding request URLs visible in browser DevTools and any proxy logs.
geocoding.corsNote: Google does not officially allow browser requests to its Geocoding API, so requests may be blocked unless served through a same-origin proxy.

ai.title: AI Assistant providers
ai.description: Configure credentials for the AI Assistant. Choose a provider, fill in the fields it needs, then Save. Only one configured provider is required to start chatting.
ai.providerHint: Choose a provider to reveal the exact fields it needs.
ai.statusReady: {{provider}} is configured and ready to use.
ai.statusIncomplete: Fill in the required fields below to enable {{provider}}.
ai.osEnvNote: The desktop app also reads matching API keys from your system environment variables. Those are used automatically and are never saved to the project file — leave a field blank to keep using the environment value.
ai.osEnvField: Read from your system environment variable {{name}}.

atmosphere.haloExtentHint: How far the atmospheric glow reaches past the globe. Lower keeps data tight to the surface; higher gives a wide, airy halo.
atmosphere.globeOnly: Atmospheric effects are only available when Globe view is enabled.

sun.title: Sun
sun.localTimeNote: Times shown in your computer's time zone.

routeAnimation.title: Route Animation
routeAnimation.noLineLayers: No line layers in the project
routeAnimation.videoError: Could not record the animation. Please try again.
routeAnimation.videoUnsupported: Video recording isn't supported in this browser.

setView.title: Set View
setView.description: Move the map to an exact camera.
setView.smartPasteInfo: Type or paste a full coordinate string in DD, DMS, or DDM, then run Process input. GeoLibre detects the format and fills the longitude and latitude fields below. Adjust zoom, pitch, and bearing separately before going.
setView.smartPasteInvalid: Could not read those coordinates. Enter a full longitude and latitude in DD, DMS, or DDM, for example 51.5074, -0.1278. A partial or incomplete string cannot be parsed.
setView.processed: Filled the longitude and latitude fields below.
setView.orientationHint: Pitch and bearing default to 0 if left blank.
setView.formatInfo:
These buttons only choose how you type the center by hand. The paste box above already accepts any format.
DD: 51.5074, -0.1278
DMS: 51°30'26"N, 0°07'39"W
DDM: 51°30.44'N, 0°07.65'W
setView.invalid: Enter a valid longitude (−180 to 180), latitude (−90 to 90), and zoom.
setView.invalidDms: Enter valid degrees, minutes (0 to 59), seconds (0 to 59), and zoom.
setView.invalidDdm: Enter valid degrees, decimal minutes (0 to < 60), and zoom.

conversion.zipInputBrowserError: Zipped Shapefiles can only be converted in the GeoLibre desktop app. In the browser, select the unzipped .shp together with its .dbf, .shx, and .prj files.
conversion.browserOutputUnsupported: In the browser, the output can be GeoJSON, CSV, GeoParquet, GeoPackage, or Shapefile (.zip). ".{{extension}}" output needs the GeoLibre desktop app.
conversion.outputExtensionRequired: Add a file extension to the output name to choose the format.

rasterTool.clientHint: Computes in your browser and adds the result to the map. For large rasters or geographic-CRS terrain, prefer the sidecar.
rasterTool.switchToClientHint: Or switch the engine to Client to run this tool in your browser.
rasterTool.useAddedLayer: Use a layer on the map
rasterTool.layerLoadError: Could not load the selected layer.
rasterTool.largeRasterWarning: Large raster ({{mp}} M pixels); the browser may be unresponsive while computing.
rasterTool.mapAddFailed: Map add failed (the result is still available to download): {{message}}
rasterTool.mapAddError: Could not add the result to the map.

item.weatherTooltip: Overlay near-realtime weather layers you can animate.
item.cloudsTooltip: Near-realtime NASA satellite clouds, like Google Earth. Animate day by day.
item.precipitationTooltip: Near-realtime RainViewer weather radar. Animate the last ~2 hours.
item.sunTooltip: Simulate the sun's position and the day/night terminator for any date and time.
item.routeAnimationTooltip: Animate a marker along a line layer, with play/pause, speed, a trail, and camera follow.
item.spinGlobeBoundsDesc: The globe can't spin while the map bounds are locked.
item.spinGlobeBoundsHint: Unlocking the bounds lets the globe spin freely. You can re-lock them anytime in Settings.
item.directionsTooltip: Routing sends your waypoints to the public OSRM demo server (router.project-osrm.org).
item.openProjectFromUrlDesc: Load a `.geolibre.json` project and add it to recent projects.
item.saveProjectAsDesc: Enter a file name. The project will download to your browser's downloads folder when you click Save.
item.embedVectorDesc: This project has {{count}} local vector layer(s) loaded from your computer. The browser cannot re-read them when the project is reopened, so their data must be embedded in the project file to be restored. Embedding adds about {{size}} to the file.
item.embedVectorDescDesktop: This project has {{count}} local vector layer(s) loaded from your computer. Embed their data to make the project self-contained (adds about {{size}}), or save file references that reload from disk when the project is reopened. References keep the file small but break if a source file is later moved or deleted.
item.exportHtmlAsDesc: Enter a file name. The HTML page will download to your browser's downloads folder when you click Save.
item.directionsNoticeTitle: Directions uses a public routing server
item.directionsNoticeDesc: Turning on Directions sends the waypoints you place to the public OSRM demo server (router.project-osrm.org) to calculate routes — your coordinates leave your device for those requests. The demo server is rate-limited and supports driving only.
item.reverseGeocodeTooltip: Click the map to look up an address. The clicked coordinates are sent to the configured geocoding provider (public Nominatim by default; change it in Settings → Geocoding).
item.reverseGeocodeNoticeTitle: Reverse Geocode uses an external service
item.reverseGeocodeNoticeDesc: Turning on Reverse Geocode sends the coordinates of each point you click to the configured geocoding provider (the public Nominatim service by default) to resolve an address — your coordinates leave your device for those requests. You can choose a different provider in Settings → Geocoding.
item.networkNoticeTitle: Network analysis uses an external routing server
item.networkNoticeDesc: Running a Network analysis tool sends the coordinates of your input points to a routing server to compute isochrones, cost matrices, and routes.
item.networkNoticePrivacy: Your input coordinates leave this device and are sent to the public Valhalla server at valhalla1.openstreetmap.de. Press Cancel if your project is bound by data privacy or data-sovereignty rules.
item.networkNoticePrivacyCustom: Your input coordinates leave this device and are sent to the configured routing server ({{endpoint}}). Press Cancel if your project is bound by data privacy or data-sovereignty rules.
item.networkNoticePerformance: The public server is free but rate-limited, so large batches of points may be throttled or fail. For heavy use, run your own Valhalla server and enter its URL in the tool's "Routing server (Valhalla)" field (or set VITE_ROUTING_ENDPOINT in Settings → Environment Variables to make it the default).
item.largeOsmPbfDesc: This file is about {{sizeMb}} MB. Parsing it converts every feature to GeoJSON in memory, which can be slow and may use a lot of memory in the browser. Continue?
item.largeVectorDesc: {{name}} has about {{count}} features. Loading it converts every feature to GeoJSON in memory and may slow down or freeze the app. Continue?
item.addOsmPbfLayerDesc: Load an OpenStreetMap .osm.pbf file. It is parsed in your browser and split into separate point, line, and polygon layers.
item.osmPbfUrlHint: Remote URLs load in the desktop app; in the browser the server must allow cross-origin requests (CORS).
item.loadingOsmPbfDesc: Parsing the file and converting features to GeoJSON. This runs in the background and may take a moment for large extracts.
item.loadingOsmPbfProgress: Converting features to GeoJSON… {{processed}} of {{total}} OSM entities processed.

fileDrop.overlayTitle: Drop vector or raster files here to add layers
fileDrop.overlaySubtext: Your data is added instantly to the Layers panel.
fileDrop.addedVectorLayers_one: Added {{count}} vector layer to the Layers panel.
fileDrop.addedVectorLayers_other: Added {{count}} vector layers to the Layers panel.
fileDrop.addedRasterLayers_one: Added {{count}} raster layer to the Layers panel.
fileDrop.addedRasterLayers_other: Added {{count}} raster layers to the Layers panel.
fileDrop.addedBoth: Added {{vector}} and {{raster}} to the Layers panel.

error.invalidProjectUrl: Enter a valid HTTP or HTTPS project URL.
error.couldNotOpenRecentProject: Could not open the recent project.
error.couldNotLoadRecentProject: Could not load the recent project.
error.couldNotExportHtml: Could not export the project to HTML.
error.osmPbfNoFeatures: No point, line, or polygon features were found in this OSM PBF file.
error.couldNotLoadOsmPbf: Could not load the OSM PBF file.
error.osmPbfLoadFailedWithHint: {{message}} If this is a Mapbox Vector Tile (.pbf), it cannot be loaded as an OSM extract.
error.osmPbfTooLarge: This OSM extract is too large to load and ran out of memory or time. Try a smaller region, or pre-filter the file first (for example with osmium-tool or QGIS) before loading it.
error.couldNotOpenOsmPbf: Could not open the OSM PBF file.
error.invalidOsmPbfUrl: Enter a valid http(s) URL to an .osm.pbf file.
error.couldNotDownloadOsmPbf: Could not download the OSM PBF file.
error.scenegraphInvalidLngLat: Enter a valid longitude and latitude.
error.scenegraphOutOfRange: Longitude must be between -180 and 180, and latitude between -90 and 90.
error.scenegraphNoPointFeatures: The GeoJSON has no point features to place models at.

description.postgis: Run PostGIS SQL against loaded layers. The PostGIS extension is loaded, so ST_* functions are available. The first run loads a ~19 MB database engine.
description.duckdb: Run DuckDB SQL against loaded layers, files, and URLs. The spatial extension is loaded, so ST_* functions are available. Cloud URLs (s3://, gs://, az://) are supported for public data.
description.sedona: Run Apache Sedona spatial SQL against loaded layers. Runs in-browser via CereusDB (a WebAssembly build of SedonaDB); on desktop it uses the SedonaDB sidecar when the optional sedona extra is installed. ST_* functions are available.

sqlWorkspace.title: SQL Workspace
sqlWorkspace.placeholder: SQL here · Ctrl/Cmd+Enter to run · Tab or Ctrl+Space to autocomplete
sqlWorkspace.resultsPlaceholder: Run a query to see results here.
sqlWorkspace.sampleForLayerLabel: Insert a sample query for a layer
sqlWorkspace.noLayersEngine: No vector layers are loaded as tables yet. Load a vector layer to query it with {{engine}}.
sqlWorkspace.noLayersReaders: No vector layers are loaded as tables yet. You can still read files and URLs with read_parquet(), read_csv_auto(), or ST_Read().
sqlWorkspace.addedAsLayer_one: Added {{count}} feature to the map as "{{name}}".
sqlWorkspace.addedAsLayer_other: Added {{count}} features to the map as "{{name}}".
sqlWorkspace.showingRows: Showing first {{shown}} of {{total}} rows. Export to see them all.

loadEditorFeatures.title: Load Features into Editor
loadEditorFeatures.description: Load the vector features visible in the current map view into the GeoEditor to edit them, then save every feature or only your changes as GeoJSON.
loadEditorFeatures.noLayers: No vector layers are in the current view. Add a vector layer or pan to a basemap area with vector features, then refresh.
loadEditorFeatures.editSessionActive: Finish the in-place geometry edit (Layers panel → Finish editing geometry) before loading or saving features here.
loadEditorFeatures.noneInView: No features found in the current view for this layer.
loadEditorFeatures.editorUnavailable: Could not activate the GeoEditor. Try again once the map has fully loaded.
loadEditorFeatures.noEditable: No editable features were found in the current view for this layer.
loadEditorFeatures.loadFailed: Failed to load features into the editor.
loadEditorFeatures.confirmExisting: The editor already holds {{count}} feature(s). Append to them, or discard and replace?
loadEditorFeatures.tooMany: Loading {{count}} features may make the editor slow to respond. Load them anyway?
loadEditorFeatures.appendHint: Add the new features alongside the existing ones.
loadEditorFeatures.discardHint: Remove the existing features and load the new ones.
loadEditorFeatures.saveChangesHint: Save only the features you added, edited, or deleted.

geocode.title: Geocode Addresses
geocode.description: Geocode a CSV of addresses into a point layer. Each matched row becomes a point carrying the original columns plus the resolved coordinates and match score.
geocode.providerHint: API keys and endpoints are managed in Settings.
geocode.apiKeyRequired: {{provider}} needs an API key. Add one in Settings to get results.
geocode.corsNote: Google does not officially allow browser requests to its Geocoding API; requests may be blocked unless served through a same-origin proxy.
geocode.rowCapWarning: Only the first {{cap}} rows will be geocoded to respect the public Nominatim usage policy. Configure a self-hosted endpoint for larger batches.
geocode.skippedEmpty: Skipped {{count}} row(s) with an empty address.
geocode.summary: Geocoded {{matched}} of {{total}} row(s); {{failed}} had no match.

mapillary.title: Mapillary
mapillary.hint: Click a coverage point on the map to view street-level imagery.
mapillary.noToken: A Mapillary access token is required to load coverage and imagery. Paste one below to get started.

openAerialMap.hint: Search OpenAerialMap imagery by map view, a drawn box, or coordinates.
openAerialMap.noResults: No imagery found in this area.
openAerialMap.searchError: Could not reach OpenAerialMap: {{message}}. Please try again.
openAerialMap.addTitle: Add this image to the map
openAerialMap.removeTitle: Remove this image from the map
openAerialMap.addUnavailableTitle: No tile service available for this image
openAerialMap.drawHint: Click and drag on the map to draw a search box.
openAerialMap.bboxInvalid: Enter four valid coordinates with west < east and south < north.
openAerialMap.footprintUnavailable: No tile service available for this image.

basemaps.confirmStyleReplace_one: Switching to "{{name}}" replaces the whole map style and will remove the stacked basemap you added. Continue?
basemaps.confirmStyleReplace_other: Switching to "{{name}}" replaces the whole map style and will remove the {{count}} stacked basemaps you added. Continue?

pythonConsole.title: Python Console
pythonConsole.placeholder: Python here · Ctrl/Cmd+Enter to run · Tab to autocomplete · ↑/↓ for history
pythonConsole.intro: Python console ready. Use the `geolibre` object to script the map, e.g. geolibre.get_center(). Tab autocompletes, ↑/↓ recall previous commands, and `await geolibre.load_package("numpy")` adds packages.
pythonConsole.loadFailed: Failed to load the Python runtime.
pythonConsole.discardChanges: Discard unsaved changes to the current script?
pythonConsole.editorPlaceholder: Write a multi-line Python script here. Ctrl/Cmd+Enter runs it (or the selection) in the console; Tab indents; Ctrl+Space autocompletes.

dashboard.title: Dashboard
dashboard.empty: No widgets yet. Add a chart to summarize a layer.
dashboard.emptyNoLayers: No compatible layers found. Add a vector or DuckDB query layer to your project using the Add Data menu above, then return here to start building charts.
dashboard.noLayersHint: Add a vector or DuckDB query layer first.
dashboard.noData: This layer has no chartable attributes.

chart.emptyScatter: No rows have both fields set to a number.

editor.description: Choose a layer, a chart type, and the fields to plot.
editor.noLayers: No chartable layers. Add a vector or DuckDB query layer first.
editor.noFields: This layer has no numeric or categorical fields to chart.

assistant.title: AI Assistant
assistant.placeholder: Ask about your data, e.g. "show countries with population over 50 million" · Ctrl/Cmd+Enter to send
assistant.intro: Chat with your data. I can run Spatial SQL, add or remove layers, restyle them, and move the map — every change is undoable. Try "color the countries by population with a graduated red ramp".
assistant.setupStatus: The assistant needs an AI provider to do the thinking. Add credentials for any one provider below and the panel switches to chat mode as soon as you save.
assistant.setupProviders: Add credentials for any one provider
assistant.codeApprovalBody: The assistant wants to run {{language}} code in the app. Review it first — it can change the map and access app data.
assistant.codeApprovalAlways: Allow assistant code for the rest of this session

storymap.title: Story Map
storymap.description: Build a scroll-driven story from map chapters. Each chapter captures a camera view and text.
storymap.empty: No chapters yet. Navigate the map, then add a chapter to capture the current view.

composeMode.title: Composing chapter
composeMode.hint: Pan, zoom, and tilt the map, then save the view to this chapter.
composeMode.save: Save view and return to Story Map
composeMode.waiting: Wait for the map to finish moving

handout.title: PDF Handout Generator
handout.description: Export selected chapter views as a multi-page PDF handout.
handout.documentTitlePlaceholder: Title shown at the top of each page
handout.subtitle: Subtitle
handout.subtitlePlaceholder: Subtitle shown under the title on each page
handout.footerPlaceholder: Footer shown at the bottom of each page
handout.noMap: The map is not ready yet. Try again in a moment.
handout.noneSelected: Select at least one screen to export.

storymap.resetTitle: Clear the story and start over
storymap.resetConfirm: Clear this story map? All chapters and settings will be removed so you can start your own.

network.title: Network analysis
network.description: Isochrones, service areas, and origin–destination cost matrices from your point layers.
network.heading: Network
network.endpointNotice: Point coordinates are sent to the routing server ({{endpoint}}). The default is a shared public Valhalla server (FOSSGIS), which is rate-limited. For heavy use, enter your own server's URL in the "Routing server (Valhalla)" field above.

statistics.title: Spatial statistics
statistics.description: Spatial autocorrelation, hotspot analysis, and density measures. Every tool runs client-side on your GeoJSON layers.
statistics.heading: Spatial Statistics

whitebox.runLocalHint: Run locally in WebAssembly (no Python sidecar)
whitebox.runningLocally: Running locally with WebAssembly. No sidecar required.
whitebox.catalogSnapshotError: Could not load Whitebox catalog snapshot.
whitebox.localRunnerError: Could not load the local (WASM) tool runner. Refresh and try again, or use the sidecar if it is available.

output.projectedHint: Saved to your downloads in the target CRS. The map only displays EPSG:4326, so a projected result is not added as a layer.

whitebox.drawBboxHint: Drag a box on the map to set the bounding box. Press Esc to cancel.
whitebox.mapExtentUnavailable: The map view is not available yet. Wait for the map to load, then try again.
whitebox.mapExtentInvalid: The current map view can't be used as a bounding box: it wraps the 180° meridian or extends beyond valid longitude/latitude bounds. Adjust the view, then try again.

sidecar.showHelp: Show what this means and how to fix it
sidecar.intro: The processing server is an optional local helper ({{sidecarUrl}}) that runs heavier Python-backed tools in the background, separate from the app. Most Whitebox tools don't need it: they can run entirely in your browser with WebAssembly.
sidecar.wasmTip: Enable "Run locally (WASM)" to run Whitebox tools in your browser. No server, install, or setup required.
sidecar.troubleshootingTitle: Still want to use the server?
sidecar.stepStartServerDesktop: Click "Start server" in the tool list panel to launch it, then wait a few seconds for it to come online.
sidecar.stepStartServerBrowser: The server can only be started from the GeoLibre desktop app. In the browser, keep "Run locally (WASM)" enabled instead.
sidecar.stepCheckPort: Make sure a firewall, antivirus, or network policy isn't blocking local port {{port}}.
sidecar.stepRestart: If the server stopped unexpectedly, restart the GeoLibre desktop app and reopen this dialog.

segmentation.title: AI Segmentation
segmentation.description: Turn imagery into vector features with SAM3 (segment-geospatial). Choose a GeoTIFF, describe what to segment, and get polygons back as a new layer.

status.unavailableDesktop: The AI segmentation service is not running yet. Start the GeoLibre server with segment-geospatial enabled to use it.
status.unavailableWeb: AI segmentation isn't available in the browser. Use the GeoLibre desktop app to run it.

error.enterPrompt: Enter a text prompt, e.g. “trees” or “buildings”.

sidecar.title: Couldn't start the AI segmentation server.
sidecar.intro: AI segmentation runs in a local helper server ({{sidecarUrl}}) that hosts the SAM3 model from segment-geospatial. It runs Python in the background, separate from the app, and isn't part of the browser build.
sidecar.troubleshootingTitle: How to get the server running
sidecar.stepInstall: Make sure the GeoLibre server is installed with the segment-geospatial backend enabled, so the segmentation model is available.
sidecar.stepStartServerDesktop: Click "Start server" above to launch it, then wait a few seconds for the model to come online.

objectDetection.title: Object Detection
objectDetection.description: Run a YOLO model exported to ONNX over a GeoTIFF, fully in the browser. Detected boxes are georeferenced and each class is added as its own layer.
objectDetection.hint: Use a built-in COCO model (downloaded and cached once) or bring your own YOLOv5/v8/v11 ONNX. Detection runs locally in the browser. Built-in models detect everyday objects; for aerial imagery, supply your own overhead-trained model.
objectDetection.noObjects: No objects detected. Try lowering the confidence threshold.

error.chooseImage: Choose an image (GeoTIFF) to run detection on.
error.chooseModel: Choose a YOLO model exported to ONNX (.onnx).
error.downloadModel: Could not download the model. Check your connection and try again.

segmentEverything.title: Segment Everything
segmentEverything.description: Run SlimSAM automatically over a GeoTIFF, fully in the browser. Every detected object becomes a georeferenced polygon added as one layer.
segmentEverything.hint: A grid of points is sampled across the image and segmented with SlimSAM (downloaded and cached once). No clicks or labels — denser grids find more objects but take longer.
segmentEverything.noObjects: No objects segmented. Try a denser grid or a lower quality threshold.

error.downloadModel: Could not download the model. Check your connection and try again.

attributeTable.title: Attribute table
attributeTable.editTitleFinishGeometry: Finish geometry editing to edit attributes
attributeTable.editTitleReadOnly: Editing is not available for Add Vector Layer layers
attributeTable.editTitleUseSaveCancel: Use Save or Cancel to finish editing
attributeTable.editTitleDuckdb: Edit displayed DuckDB query attributes in memory
attributeTable.fieldCalculatorTitle: Calculate field values from an expression
attributeTable.columnExplorerTitle: Explore all fields at a glance
attributeTable.columnExplorerTitleDisabled: Column explorer requires a vector or DuckDB query layer
attributeTable.statisticsTitleDisabled: Statistics require a vector or DuckDB query layer
attributeTable.chartsTitleDisabled: Charts require a vector or DuckDB query layer
attributeTable.dashboardTitle: Open the dashboard to build chart widgets
attributeTable.requiresVectorLayer: Attribute table requires a vector or DuckDB query layer.
attributeTable.deleteFieldConfirm: This permanently removes the field "{{field}}" from every feature in "{{layer}}". Styling or labels that reference it will be cleared. This cannot be undone.
attributeTable.addFieldDescription: Add a new field to every feature in "{{layer}}".
attributeTable.noFeaturesForField: This layer has no features to add a field to.
attributeTable.calcDescription: Compute values from a JavaScript expression and write them to a field in "{{layer}}".
attributeTable.calcExpressionPlaceholder: e.g. pop / area, or upper(name), or pop > 100 ? "big" : "small"
attributeTable.sampleRowErrored: Sample row errored (other rows may still compute): {{message}}
attributeTable.calcCouldNotApply: Could not apply the calculation to this layer.
attributeTable.calcAppliedWithErrors: Applied, but {{errors}} of {{evaluated}} feature(s) errored and were written as null.
attributeTable.exportFailed: Could not export the selected layer.

style.heading: Style

raster.greyscaleHint: Toggle a greyscale basemap (drives saturation to its minimum)
raster.resetHint: Reset adjustments to their default values
raster.exactValueHint: Double-click to enter an exact value
raster.footerDeck: Changes apply live to the raster layer opacity.
raster.footerMaplibre: Changes apply live to MapLibre raster paint properties.

visibility.minZoomTooltip: Zoom level at which this layer appears. Higher numbers are more zoomed in.
visibility.maxZoomTooltip: Zoom level at which this layer disappears. Higher numbers are more zoomed in.

labels.heading: Labels
labels.dedupeHint: Collapses points stacked at the same coordinate. Uses the label field on local point layers.
labels.expressionHint: A MapLibre expression (JSON). Overrides the field when set.
labels.expressionInvalid: Not a valid MapLibre expression (must be a JSON array). This value is ignored.

elevation3d.verticalScaleTooltip: Multiplies each coordinate's Z value. 1 renders true elevations; larger values emphasize relief.
elevation3d.offsetTooltip: Constant height in meters added to every feature, e.g. to lift a track above 3D buildings or terrain.
elevation3d.footer: Rendered in 3D at coordinate Z values via deck.gl. Changes apply live. Identify and feature highlighting are not available in this mode.

symbology.noRulesPrefix: No rules yet. Add a rule and give it a MapLibre filter expression (e.g.
symbology.noRulesSuffix: ). The first matching rule wins; the Else rule colors everything else.
symbology.ruleColorPick: Pick rule {{index}} color from the screen
symbology.filterInvalid: Invalid filter — must be a JSON array expression. This rule is skipped until fixed.
symbology.elseRuleColorPick: Pick the else rule color from the screen
symbology.applyHint: Click Apply to activate rule-based rendering.
symbology.transparentSwatch: No color (transparent). Click to choose a color.
symbology.classColorPick: Pick class {{index}} color from the screen
symbology.errorChooseAttribute: Choose an attribute for this style mode.
symbology.errorGraduatedStops: Graduated style requires at least two numeric stops.
symbology.errorCategorizedStops: Categorized style requires at least one category.

style.selectLayerHint: Select a layer to edit its style.
style.noControls: Style controls are not available for this layer type yet.
style.footerDeck: Changes apply live to DuckDB deck.gl layer styling.
style.footerMaplibre: Changes apply live to MapLibre paint properties.

expressionErrors.notArray: {{label}} must be a JSON array expression.
expressionErrors.notOperator: {{label}} must start with an operator, e.g. ["==", ["get", "field"], value].

layers.identifyUnavailable: Identify is only available for vector, WMS, and COG layers
layers.exportStyleDataNotReady: Layer data is not ready yet. Try again in a moment.
layers.exportStyleNeedsFeatures: Style export requires a vector layer with features.
layers.exportStyleError: Could not export this layer's style.
layers.importStyleInvalid: That file is not valid JSON, SLD, or QML.
layers.importStyleNoMatch: No symbology could be read from that style.
layers.bindDialogDescription: Filter this layer's features by a date or timestamp property as you scrub the timeline. The layer keeps its styling and opacity.
layers.bindScanning: Scanning features for date and timestamp properties…
layers.bindNoProperty: No date or timestamp property was found in this layer.
layers.bindNoTimestamps: The selected property has no readable timestamps. Pick a different property.
layers.bindWindowWide: Within one step before and after
layers.bindWindowWider: Within three steps before and after
layers.saveEditsSuccess: Saved {{count}} feature(s) to the source file.
layers.saveEditsNoFeatures: This layer has no features to save.
layers.saveEditsError: Could not save edits to the source file.
layers.saveEditsPostgisSkippedFields: Fields without a matching table column were not saved: {{fields}}.
layers.saveEditsPostgisRefreshWarning: Edits were saved to the table, but refreshing the layer failed. Reload the layer before saving again, or new features may be inserted twice.
layers.saveEditsPostgisNoConnection: No PostgreSQL connection is available for this layer. Reconnect in Add Data > PostgreSQL, then try again.
layers.opacityExactHint: Double-click to enter an exact value
layers.hiddenByGroup: Hidden because its group is not visible
layers.empty: No data layers. Add data from the toolbar.
layers.emptyBeginner: No layers yet. Add basemaps via Plugins → Basemaps, or your own data via the Add Data menu.
layers.watchError: Could not watch this file for changes.
layers.autoRefreshDialogDescription: Reload this layer from its source on a fixed interval.
layers.enterPositiveSeconds: Enter a positive number of seconds.
layers.removeLayerConfirmBody: This removes {{name}} from the project and map.
layers.refreshVectorControlError: Could not refresh this layer. Try re-opening the Add Vector Layer panel.
layers.exportNeedsFeatures: Export requires a vector layer with features.

rasterSymbology.customColorsValid: {{count}} colors, low to high. Use Reverse to flip.
rasterSymbology.customColorsHint: Enter at least two hex codes, separated by commas or spaces.
rasterSymbology.createLegendHint: Adds a legend with one item per pixel value, using the raster's palette colors. Rename items to class names afterward.
rasterSymbology.createLegendEmpty: No palette colors found for this raster.
rasterSymbology.createLegendError: Could not read the raster palette.

raster.cogConvertConfirm: "{{name}}" is a plain (striped) GeoTIFF, which cannot be displayed directly. Convert it to a Cloud-Optimized GeoTIFF now and load that?
raster.cogConvertRemoteConfirm: "{{name}}" is a plain (striped) GeoTIFF, which cannot be displayed directly. Displaying it means downloading the full file and converting it to a Cloud-Optimized GeoTIFF in the browser, which can be slow and memory-intensive for large files. Proceed?
raster.cogConvertLargeConfirm: "{{name}}" is a large {{width}}×{{height}} GeoTIFF that is not a Cloud-Optimized GeoTIFF. Converting it in the browser may be slow and memory-intensive. Convert and load it anyway?
raster.cogConvertFailed: Could not convert "{{name}}" to a Cloud-Optimized GeoTIFF. Try converting it with gdal_translate or rio cogeo, then load the result.
raster.rasterDownloadFailed: Could not download "{{name}}". The server may be slow, unreachable, or blocking cross-origin requests, or the file may be too large to hold in memory. Check the URL and try again.
raster.rasterNotGeotiff: "{{name}}" did not return a GeoTIFF. The server may have sent an error or login page instead. Check the URL and try again.
